title: STANDARD FOR DRIED EDIBLE FUNGI CXS 39-1981 Adopted in 1981. Amended in 2022, 2023.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR DRIED EDIBLE FUNGI CXS 39-1981 Adopted in 1981. Amended in 2022, 2023.
sections.summary: Title of the Codex standard specifying the Standard for Dried Edible Fungi (CXS 39‑1981), adopted in 1981 and amended in 2022 and 2023.
2022 amendment: Section 6.2 on labelling of non‑retail containers was added to the standard.
2023 amendment: the wording of Section 7 (Methods of analysis and sampling) was amended.
Scope: the standard applies to dried (including freeze‑dried), whole or sliced edible fungi of all species after preparation and packaging.
Section heading introducing the product description section (no substantive content in the heading itself).

Defines product forms: whole dried fungi, whole caps without stems, cut dried fungi (slices 1–4 mm), and permits other styles provided they are distinctive, meet relevant standard requirements (including defect limits and drained weight), and are adequately labelled to avoid consumer confusion.
Whole dried fungi are defined as cleaned and dried edible fungi, with stalks permitted to be shortened.
Section heading for the 'whole caps without stems' presentation style; no substantive definition text provided here.

sections.section: 2.1.3 Cut dried fungi : the product obtained from whole edible fungi sliced and dried, the thickness of individual slices being 1-4 mm.
sections.summary: Cut dried fungi are defined as whole edible fungi sliced and dried with individual slice thickness of 1–4 mm.

Other presentation styles are permitted if they are sufficiently distinctive, comply with the standard’s relevant requirements (e.g., defect limits, drained weight), and are adequately described on the label to avoid misleading consumers.

Defines specific defect terms for dried edible fungi — damaged, carbonized, maggot‑damaged, seriously maggot‑damaged, crushed, fallen‑off stalks, organic (vegetable) impurities and mineral impurities — and gives brief criteria for each.
Defines 'damaged fungi' as whole fungi with more than one quarter of the cap missing, or for cut fungi, slices with more than one third of the total surface missing.
Defines 'carbonized fungi' as whole or cut dried fungi showing traces of carbonization on their surface.
Defines 'maggot‑damaged fungi' as fungi having holes caused by maggots.
Defines 'seriously maggot‑damaged fungi' as fungi having four or more holes caused by maggots.
Defines 'crushed fungi' as parts of mushrooms that pass through a sieve with a 5 x 5 mm mesh.
Defines 'fallen‑off stalks' as stalks separated from the caps.
Defines 'organic impurities of vegetable origin' as admixtures of other edible fungi or plant parts such as leaves, pine needles, etc., in the product.
Defines 'mineral impurities' as substances that, after ashing, remain as insoluble residues in hydrochloric acid.
Specifies the main species as all edible fungi permitted for sale by the competent authorities in the consuming countries.
Section heading only (Essential Quality Factors) and contains no substantive provisions by itself.
Requires raw material for dried edible fungi production to meet the General Standard for Edible Fungi and Fungus Products (CXS 38‑1981).

Specifies end‑product quality for dried edible fungi — healthy (not spoiled), appropriate colour/flavour/taste for the species, clean (free of organic and mineral impurities), largely free from maggot/insect damage and undamaged — and sets maximum moisture contents of 6% m/m for freeze‑dried, 12% m/m for other dried fungi and 13% m/m for dried shiitake.
Sets maximum water content limits for dried edible fungi: freeze‑dried 6% m/m, dried (other than freeze‑dried) 12% m/m, and dried shiitake 13% m/m.

Allows up to 25% m/m of fungi not meeting end‑product requirements and prescribes individual tolerances: mineral impurities ≤2% m/m; organic vegetable impurities ≤0.02% m/m (≤1% m/m for shiitake); maggot damage — wild fungi ≤20% m/m (including serious), cultivated fungi ≤1% m/m total (≤0.5% m/m serious); crushed ≤6% m/m; carbonized ≤2% m/m; damaged ≤20% m/m; and fallen‑off stalks must equal number of caps (1:1).

Provides hygiene provisions and recommendations for dried edible fungi: follow relevant Codex food hygiene codes and good manufacturing practice, keep product free from objectionable matter where possible, and ensure by appropriate testing the product is free from microorganisms, parasites, or microbial substances in amounts that may represent a health hazard.

sections.section: 4.1 It  is  recommended that  the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), 2  and other codes of practice recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission which are relevant to this product.
sections.summary: Recommends preparing and handling the product in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1‑1969) and other Codex codes of practice relevant to dried edible fungi.

sections.section: 4.2 To the extent possible in good manufacturing practice, the product shall be free from objectionable matter.
sections.summary: Requires, to the extent possible under good manufacturing practice, that the product be free from objectionable matter.

sections.section: 4.3 When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination, the product:
sections.summary: Specifies that when tested by appropriate sampling and examination the product must be free from microorganisms, parasites, and substances originating from microorganisms in amounts that may represent a hazard to health.
Section heading only (Packaging and Presentation) with no substantive provisions included in this entry.
Each package in a lot must contain fungi of the same commercial type and all packages must have a uniform net weight.

Cartons, bags and boxes must protect against humidity during storage/transport; inner materials must be new, waterproof and harmless to health; fungi must not contact printed inscriptions on the package, and fungi shall be loosely packed in packing units.
Introductory provision stating that the following specific labelling rules supplement the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985).

Label must specify the common and scientific species name (or customary local genus/species term), the product type ('dried fungi' or 'freeze-dried fungi') and the style ('whole', 'caps' or 'sliced'); if produced in other styles the label must include additional wording near the product name to avoid misleading consumers.
Labelling of non-retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

Compliance with this standard must be checked using the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions concerned.
